# Feature Request Template – ArchSpine

## Overview
This document is the standard feature request template used across the ArchSpine project. It provides a structured form for contributors and users to propose new capabilities or improvements to existing workflows.

## Purpose
The template exists to ensure every feature suggestion follows a consistent format. By requiring clear descriptions of the problem, the proposed solution, and any alternatives considered, it makes it easier for maintainers to evaluate, discuss, and prioritize requests fairly.

## Audience
Anyone who wants to suggest a new feature or workflow enhancement for ArchSpine should use this template. This includes external contributors, community members, and internal team members filing ideas through the issue tracker.

## Key Workflows and Decisions
- **Submitting a feature request**: Fill in the required fields – “Problem to solve”, “Proposed solution”, and “Alternatives considered” – to provide actionable information.
- **Triage and prioritization**: Maintainers review submitted templates to decide which features to implement, how to scope them, and how they align with the project roadmap.
- **Discussion anchor**: The template fields serve as a structured starting point for further technical discussion and design decisions.
